title: ranunculus mancus
intro: Ranunculus mancus, also known as lesser celandine, is a perennial plant that belongs to the buttercup family and is commonly found in wetlands and along streams. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Ranunculus mancus include reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of colds and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
